name: kipping muscle up
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and use a swinging motion to generate momentum.
As you swing forward, pull your chest towards the bar, using your lats and biceps to initiate the movement.
Continue the upward motion until your chest reaches the bar, then transition into a dip position by pushing down on the bar and extending your arms.
Lower yourself back down to the starting position by bending your arms and controlling the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Engage your core and use a swinging motion to generate momentum. As you swing forward, pull your chest towards the bar, using your lats and biceps to initiate the movement. Continue the upward motion until your chest reaches the bar, then transition into a dip position by pushing down on the bar and extending your arms. Lower yourself back down to the starting position by bending your arms and controlling the descent. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
